topic_coverage.quoted_user_text: Bootstrap automated test harness so CI and local dev can verify categorization pipeline safely: runnable tests/run-tests.sh on Linux, #resolveCategory precedence coverage, runbook TEST_COMMAND wired, npm test delegation.

src/App.js (#resolveCategory pipeline), FireflyService and OpenAiService mocks, tests/ directory (new), tests/run-tests.sh and optional run-tests.ps1, docs/engineering/runbook.md TEST_COMMAND, package.json npm test script, GitHub Actions checks job.

No live Firefly/OpenAI integration tests (scope_out). node:test or lightweight framework only. US-0003 blocked until US-0001 ships. Runbook cites missing tests/run-tests.ps1 (R6); Linux shell runner is primary AC-1 target. Brownfield monolithic App.js — mock services not full HTTP stack.
topic_coverage.delegation_rationale: Prior product backfill intake (2026-06-12) established US-0001 scope, risks, and dependency chain; drain-advance confirms unchanged constraints.

topic_coverage.quoted_user_text: AC-1: tests/run-tests.sh executable exits 0 on Linux CI. AC-2: ≥4 #resolveCategory precedence cases (account mapping beats auto-cat beats AI mock). AC-3: runbook TEST_COMMAND points to working runner. AC-4: npm test delegates. AC-5: CI checks job runs TEST_COMMAND.
Done when all US-0001 acceptance rows pass: Linux runner green, precedence tests in place, runbook and npm test aligned, CI workflow reports pass/fail from TEST_COMMAND.

decomposition.rationale: US-0001 remains a single quality-engineering vertical slice; no split on drain-advance.
decomposition.alternative_considered: Split runner vs unit tests — rejected; runner and precedence tests ship together per original decomposition.
